fixedSinceLastReview: Nothing. Absolutely nothing. We are now on our fourth consecutive review where 15+ open items have been copy-pasted forward untouched. At this rate, the bugs will outlive the product.

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- Version drift: source v1.0.0-chaos, live site v1.0.5-chaos
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEW #10/#9/#8 -- App.jsx line 1190: `v1.0.0-chaos`. Mobile screenshot CONFIRMS: live footer reads 'v1.0.5-chaos'. That is a 5-patch gap between source and deployment, and it has been this way for at least 4 review cycles. Either (a) /workspace/src is perpetually stale and QA has been auditing the wrong code for months, or (b) live patches are hand-applied without committing. Both are catastrophic for process hygiene. Fix: (1) Run `git log --oneline` on the deployed branch, sync /workspace/src to HEAD. (2) Inject version from package.json at build time: replace the hardcoded string with `import.meta.env.VITE_APP_VERSION` and in vite.config.js add `define: { 'import.meta.env.VITE_APP_VERSION': JSON.stringify(pkg.version) }`. One-time setup, prevents this forever.

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- updated_at used for WorkerCard duration badge AND StatsBar stuck detection -- always <=30s, never triggers warn/stuck
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEW #7/#8/#9/#10. (1) WorkerCard duration badge (App.jsx lines 303-306): `durationMs = Date.now() - new Date(worker.updated_at).getTime()` -- if updated_at refreshes on every API poll, durationMs is always <=30,000ms, `tier` is permanently 'normal', the stuck badge never fires. Fix: replace `worker.updated_at` with `worker.startedAt` on lines 303, 304, and 306. (2) StatsBar stuck detection (App.jsx line 386): `Date.now() - new Date(w.updated_at).getTime() > 7_200_000` -- a genuinely stuck worker whose updated_at refreshes silently will never appear in the stuck list. Fix: change `w.updated_at` to `w.startedAt` at line 386. Also line 457: clean up `stuckWorkers[0].updated_at || stuckWorkers[0].startedAt` fallback -- once line 386 is fixed, flip the order to `stuckWorkers[0].startedAt || stuckWorkers[0].updated_at`.

suggestions.title: NEW -- char--deep-work detection AND ghost desk detection also use updated_at -- both permanently broken
suggestions.description: NEW IN REVIEW #11. Two more instances of the updated_at family of bugs missed in previous reviews. (1) Office.jsx line 400: `if (variant === 'working' && worker.updated_at && Date.now() - new Date(worker.updated_at).getTime() > 45 * 60 * 1000)` -- sets the `char--deep-work` CSS class for workers in 45+ minutes of focused work. If updated_at resets on poll, this threshold never fires: no character ever enters the deep-work animation state. Fix: replace `worker.updated_at` with `worker.startedAt`. (2) Office.jsx lines 812-813: `isGhostDesk = isWorking && occ.worker.updated_at && (Date.now() - new Date(occ.worker.updated_at).getTime()) > 900000` -- ghost desk visual (desk goes translucent after 15min) also uses updated_at. Same failure mode: never fires. Fix: replace `occ.worker.updated_at` with `occ.worker.startedAt` at line 812. The total updated_at/startedAt confusion count is now FOUR distinct broken features across two files.

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- React.memo on WorkerCard defeated by stableActivityLog + side effect inside useMemo
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEW #7/#8/#9/#10. App.jsx lines 763-767: `stableActivityLog = useMemo(() => { activityLogLenRef.current = activityLog.length; return activityLog }, [activityLog.length])`. Two problems: (1) Any new activity (length changes) invalidates stableActivityLog, returning a new array reference -- ALL WorkerCards re-render because activityEntries={stableActivityLog} propagates to every card (lines 1078, 1101, 1114). React.memo buys zero benefit. Fix: `const workerActivitySlices = useMemo(() => Object.fromEntries(activeWorkers.map(w => [w.id, activityLog.filter(e => e.worker === w.name).slice(-3).reverse()])), [activityLog, activeWorkers])` and pass `activityEntries={workerActivitySlices[w.id] ?? []}` per card. (2) Line 765: `activityLogLenRef.current = activityLog.length` inside useMemo is a textbook React violation -- side effects in useMemo are explicitly forbidden. The ref is never read in the render path. Delete it.

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- Header greeting badge overflows off-screen when vibe is slow-day
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEW #9/#10. Currently masked because vibe is 'in-flow' (honorific = 'boss', 4 chars) -- badge fits fine on mobile as confirmed in this screenshot. But App.jsx line 922: `HONORIFICS['slow-day'] = 'chief... you still there?'`. The full badge text at slow-day becomes 'Good morning, chief... you still there? -- Sunday, Mar 8' -- ~380px at 0.72rem on a 375px viewport. App.css line 61: `white-space: nowrap` on `.header-badge` with no `max-width` or `overflow: hidden`. It will clip the left edge the next time slow-day triggers. Fix: in App.css at the <=420px breakpoint add `.header-badge { max-width: min(280px, 100%); overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; }`. Optionally shorten the honorific in App.jsx line 922 to `'slow-day': 'chief'`.

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- VibeSparkline reads localStorage BEFORE parent writes -- sparkline permanently one vibe behind
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEW #7/#8/#9/#10. StatsBar's useEffect (App.jsx lines 398-411) reads vibeHistory from localStorage on every vibe.key change. App.jsx's vibe effect (lines 841-868) writes a new entry. React child useEffects run before parent useEffects in the same commit -- StatsBar reads the OLD value before App.jsx writes the new entry. The sparkline permanently lags by one vibe state. Fix: lift vibeHistory state to App.jsx. In the vibe effect at ~line 862, after localStorage.setItem, also call `setVibeHistory(history)`. Pass `vibeHistory` as prop to StatsBar. Remove the localStorage-reading useEffect from StatsBar entirely (lines 398-411).

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- Office.css media query ordering bug -- 768px block overrides 600px block at mobile -- confirmed in screenshot
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEW #8/#9/#10. VISUALLY CONFIRMED in mobile screenshot: large blank area (~180px) below the office floor. Root cause: @media (max-width: 600px) at line 794 sets `.office-floor { min-height: 320px }`, then @media (max-width: 768px) at line 831 sets `.office-floor { min-height: 400px; aspect-ratio: auto }`, then @media (max-width: 480px) at line 853 sets `min-height: 280px`. At 375px ALL THREE match. Later source order wins -- the 768px block overrides the 600px one. `aspect-ratio: auto` from the 768px block is never overridden, creating the dead space. Fix: reorder rules largest-to-smallest breakpoint in source: 768px first, then 600px, then 480px.

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- ShortcutToast untouchable on mobile AND clips beyond viewport -- confirmed in screenshot
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEW #8/#9/#10. VISUALLY CONFIRMED in mobile screenshot: toast text is truncated on the left and overlaps the footer content. App.css line 1057: `pointer-events: none` -- still there, still untappable, still non-dismissable for 7 full seconds on touch devices. Fix: (1) Remove `pointer-events: none` from App.css. (2) Add `onClick={() => dismiss(true)}` to the toast div at App.jsx line 564. (3) Add to App.css: `@media (max-width: 480px) { .shortcut-toast { right: 0.5rem; left: 0.5rem; white-space: normal; text-align: center; font-size: 0.68rem; } }` so it wraps instead of clipping.

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- StatsBar stuck worker scroll uses document.querySelector -- silently fails from Office tab
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEW #7/#8/#9/#10. App.jsx line 451: `document.querySelector('[data-worker-name="${name}"]')?.scrollIntoView(...)`. WorkerCard elements only exist in the DOM when the Dashboard tab is active. Clicking the stuck worker card from the Office tab does nothing. Fix: pass `onWorkerClick` as a prop to StatsBar from App.jsx line 1057. In the onClick at line 449, call `onWorkerClick(stuckWorkers[0])` instead of the querySelector hack. `handleWorkerClick` at App.jsx line 660 already handles tab switching and WorkerCard's useEffect at lines 243-247 already handles scrollIntoView on focus.

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- Whiteboard renders 'FL OW' on mobile -- confirmed in screenshot
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEW #7/#8/#9/#10. VISUALLY CONFIRMED in mobile screenshot: whiteboard clearly shows 'FL OW' on two lines -- the vibe text is being broken mid-word. Caused by the media query ordering bug (768px `aspect-ratio: auto` never overridden), which compresses the office floor and wraps the whiteboard text. Fix priorities: (1) Fix the media query order bug first (separate item). (2) Add `white-space: nowrap; overflow: hidden;` to the `.office-whiteboard` or `.wb-vibe-text` element in Office.css. (3) Consider shortening the vibe labels for the whiteboard (Office.jsx imports `VIBE_WHITEBOARD` from constants -- the label for 'in-flow' is 'FLOW' which is exactly where the wrap occurs).

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- document.title cycles every 8s -- disruptive for screen readers
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEW #8/#9/#10. App.jsx lines 888-910: title rotates among 3 frames every 8s using setInterval. Some screen readers announce tab title changes on every cycle. No prefers-reduced-motion guard, no document.hasFocus() check. Fix A (recommended): `document.title = frames[0]` once per data change, remove the setInterval entirely -- the status info already lives in the StatsBar. Fix B: wrap the setInterval in `if (document.hasFocus() && !window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches)` and clear on visibilitychange.

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- completionToast rendered with 15 inline styles -- unthemeable, hardcodes shadow
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEW #8/#9/#10. App.jsx lines 950-973: the completion toast carries 15 inline style properties. `boxShadow` hardcodes `rgba(0,0,0,0.4)` which looks wrong in light theme. Only `border` is genuinely dynamic. Fix: extract all static properties to `.completion-toast` class in App.css. Use one CSS custom property for the dynamic value: `style={{ '--toast-border': completionToast.color }}` and in CSS: `border: 2px solid var(--toast-border)`. Separately, the element uses `aria-live='polite'` which is good, but add `role='status'` explicitly for wider screen reader compat.

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- getRelativeTime duplicated verbatim between App.jsx and Office.jsx
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEW #7/#8/#9/#10. Office.jsx lines 432-441 contain an inline IIFE with identical mins/hours/days arithmetic to `getRelativeTime()` at App.jsx lines 34-43. Byte-for-byte duplicates. Fix: create `src/utils/time.js`, export `getRelativeTime` from it, import it in both App.jsx and Office.jsx. Remove the IIFE from Office.jsx line 432 and the inline function from App.jsx lines 34-43. This eliminates the maintenance trap where future time format changes must be applied in two places.

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- Idle wander and cloud-drift animations use left/top -- layout-triggering at 60fps
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEW #9/#10 (wander) and #8/#9/#10 (cloud-drift). (1) Office.css lines 706-731: all four `@keyframes wander-N` animate `left` and `top` percentage values, forcing browser layout (reflow) on every animation frame. With up to 4 wandering characters simultaneously, this is 4x layout-triggering at 60fps on mobile. Fix: set base `left`/`top` on each `.char--wander-N` class and animate `transform: translate(Xpx, Ypx)` using delta offsets from the base position. (2) Office.css lines 783-786: `@keyframes cloud-drift { from { left: -20%; } to { left: 115%; } }` -- same problem. Fix: set `.window-cloud { left: -20%; }` as base and animate `transform: translateX(0)` to `transform: translateX(675%)`. Both are GPU-composited and eliminate layout pressure.

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- Fading worker cards all get index={activeWorkers.length} -- stagger animation lost for simultaneous departures
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEW #7/#8/#9/#10. App.jsx line 1112: `index={activeWorkers.length}` passed to EVERY fading WorkerCard in the fadingMap loop. When 2+ workers depart simultaneously, all share the same `--i` CSS variable, all fade at identical timing, stagger is lost. Fix: change `Object.values(fadingMap).map((w) => ...)` to `Object.values(fadingMap).map((w, fadingIdx) => ...)` and pass `index={activeWorkers.length + fadingIdx}`.

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- ActivityLogEntry key uses string concatenation without delimiters -- silent collision risk
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEW #8/#9/#10. App.jsx line 130: `key={entry.timestamp + entry.worker + i}`. Numeric string concatenation is collision-prone: timestamp='123', worker='45', i=0 gives '1234500'; timestamp='12345', worker='0', i=0 gives the same '1234500'. Silent React key collision causes reconciliation issues. Fix: use template literal with dash separators: `key={entry.timestamp + '-' + (entry.worker ?? 'system') + '-' + i}`.

suggestions.title: STILL OPEN #10 -- 19 ambient particle DOM nodes with permanent CSS animations compound mobile jank
suggestions.description: STILL OPEN FROM REVIEW #7/#8/#9/#10. App.jsx lines 926-930: 12 ambient particles. Office.jsx lines 994-1003: 7 more. All run permanent CSS animations. Combined with 4x layout-triggering wander animations and cloud-drift on mobile, compositor layer pressure is significant on mid-range devices during the 30s sync cycle. Fix: add `@media (pointer: coarse) { .ambient-particle:nth-child(even) { display: none; } }` in both App.css and Office.css to halve particle count on touch devices. Or consolidate both layers into one shared component capped at 8 particles.

suggestions.title: NEW -- completionToast.color hex alpha suffix '55' is fragile -- silently breaks for non-6-digit colors
suggestions.description: NEW IN REVIEW #11. App.jsx line 964: `boxShadow: '... 0 0 12px ' + completionToast.color + '55'` -- appending the string '55' to a hex color assumes the color is always a 6-digit #rrggbb hex. WORKER_ROLE_COLORS (App.jsx lines 14-20) are currently all 6-digit hex so this works today. But if anyone adds a worker with a 3-digit hex (#rgb), a named color, or an rgb() value, the shadow value becomes invalid CSS and is silently dropped. Fix: use `color-mix(in srgb, ${completionToast.color} 33%, transparent)` instead of the manual alpha suffix. Or adopt the CSS custom property pattern recommended in the completionToast inline-styles item.
